parsed_review.summary: CP00-463 is a descriptor-only RP15 analytics-core closeout pack (150 units, RP15.P04.M06.S06-RP15.P05.M04.S07, risk C). The pack-level claims are supported and correctly bounded: manifest declares production_ready=false / status=implemented_pending_review / runtime_ready=false / implementation_layer=descriptor, so nothing is over-claimed; the Hermes packet only sets production_ready_candidate gated on coverage.valid && slice.valid. Coverage is sound: registry ANALYTICS_CORE_CP463_REQUIREMENTS deliverable/phase/micro-phase/micro-title distributions match the prompt baseline exactly and total 150; validators enforce first/last unit, RP15-only, no duplicate IDs, and per-section row titles; model.test.js asserts coverage, descriptor-only rows, evidence packets, and attestations; command-evidence.json records all implementation/plan/repo-wide gates plus npm test, build, and git diff --check at exit 0. Every descriptor row is descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false, and the no-write attestation chain (CP463 spreading CP462..CP453 base) keeps every write/dispatch/leak flag false with synthetic_only/no_real_data true. Authority boundary holds across registry, service, validators, contract, and manifest: claude_is_final_approval:false, read_only:true, allowed tools Read/Grep/Glob only, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false. Review hardening is satisfied: readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1432-byte valid JSON, non-empty), and forbidden_review_evidence encodes the CP178+ rejection rules (auth failure, zero-byte, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields, plus claude_final_approval_claim/source_mutation_claim/production_enterprise_trust_claim). Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic descriptor evidence packets/receipts (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, receipt_shape descriptor_only..._no_write; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false). Claude correctly remains a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approver. Downstream artifacts referenced by the manifest (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, raw-output.json, review-receipt.json) are not yet present, which is expected since they are produced after this review step in the hardened sequence; not treated as findings. One non-blocking P3 metadata inconsistency in the review harness scope file is noted below. No P0/P1/P2 issues found.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies the pack's own artifact dir and handoff doc as both implementation and unrelated dirty files
findings.details: In changed-file-scope.json, both 'docs/closeout-packs/cp00-463/' and 'docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md' appear simultaneously in untracked_implementation_files (lines 131-132) AND in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 137-138). The pack's own artifact directory docs/closeout-packs/cp00-463/ (manifest.json + command-evidence.json) is part of this pack and should not be labeled 'unrelated'. The manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json lines 6017-6025) is correct and excludes both entries, so the discrepancy is isolated to the harness-generated scope file and does not affect pack deliverables. The active_pack_implementation_refs (all tracked+modified analytics sources) are scoped correctly, and the unrelated docx/workbook/LDIP files were correctly NOT treated as part of the pack.
findings.recommendation: Align changed-file-scope.json with the manifest worktree policy: remove the pack's own artifact directory (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-463/) and the planning handoff doc from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved so a file is not classified as both an implementation file and an unrelated dirty file. Cosmetic harness-metadata cleanup only.
findings.post_review_disposition: non_blocking_informational; surfaced for human adjudication / harness scope-file cleanup; does not block pack or goal closeout
